events.title: Half Moon Bay 4th of July Pancake Breakfast 2026

events.title: Foster City 4th of July Celebration 2026 at Leo Ryan Park
events.description: Foster City's free Fourth of July celebration returns to Leo J. Ryan Memorial Park on July 4, 2026 with a Rotary Club pancake breakfast, live music, and the popular Family & Dog Parade. Daytime festivities run through 4 p.m. Note: there is no fireworks show in 2026 due to community center construction.

events.title: 4th of July on the Wharf 2026 - Old Fisherman's Wharf Monterey
events.description: Spend the Fourth of July at Monterey's Old Fisherman's Wharf, decked out in festive red, white, and blue decor for fun family photo ops. Stroll the wharf, shop, dine, watch for whales, and soak up the holiday on Monterey Bay. A free, family-friendly way to celebrate July 4th on the coast.

events.title: Danville Kiwanis 4th of July Parade 2026
events.description: One of the Bay Area's largest Independence Day celebrations returns for its 51st year. Families line Hartz Avenue for the free Danville Kiwanis July 4th Parade with marching bands, floats, scouts, and classic cars. Arrive early for a great curbside spot.
events.venue: Downtown Danville (Hartz Avenue & San Ramon Valley Boulevard)

events.title: Alameda 4th of July Parade 2026 (Longest in the Nation)
events.description: Celebrate Independence Day at Alameda's famous Fourth of July Parade, one of the longest in the nation, stepping off at 10 a.m. from Park Street and Lincoln Avenue. Families line the 3.3-mile route for marching bands, floats, antique cars, equestrians and community groups. Free to watch.
events.venue: Park Street (start: Park St & Lincoln Ave)

events.title: Concord 4th of July Parade 2026 at Todos Santos Plaza
events.description: Concord's free Independence Day Parade marches through historic downtown around Todos Santos Plaza, kicking off a full day of all-ages July 4th festivities. The morning also features a Stars & Stripes kids fun run, 5K, and pancake breakfast.

events.title: Fillmore Jazz Festival 2026 - Free SF Street Festival (Saturday, July 4)
events.description: The Fillmore Jazz Festival returns July 4-5, 2026, as one of San Francisco's largest free outdoor music festivals. Stroll Fillmore Street between Eddy and Jackson for live jazz on multiple stages, arts and crafts vendors, and family-friendly food in the historic Fillmore District, 10am to 6pm.

events.title: Fremont 4th of July Parade 2026 Downtown
events.description: Fremont's free Fourth of July Parade rolls down Paseo Padre Parkway in downtown, celebrating America's 250th and Fremont's 70th anniversary. Bring the family for floats, bands, and a community after-party at the Downtown Event Center.

events.title: Martinez 4th of July Parade 2026 Downtown Main Street
events.description: Celebrate America's 250th and Martinez's 150th anniversary at the free Independence Day Parade down historic Main Street. Families enjoy marching units, civic groups, classic vehicles, and community floats in downtown Martinez.
events.venue: Downtown Martinez Main Street (Court St & Main St)

events.title: Morgan Hill Freedom Fest Independence Day Parade 2026 (July 4)
events.description: One of Northern California's biggest hometown Fourth of July parades, the Morgan Hill Freedom Fest parade kicks off at 10:00 AM through downtown Morgan Hill. A free, family-friendly July 4th tradition with floats, marching bands and community pride.

events.title: Novato 4th of July Parade 2026 & Buckaroo Pancake Breakfast
events.description: The free Novato 4th of July Parade 2026 marches down Grant Avenue with more than one hundred entries, making it one of the largest Independence Day parades in the Bay Area. Arrive early for the Buckaroo Pancake Breakfast (7-10 a.m.) and free pre-parade entertainment starting at 9 a.m. A beloved family-friendly July 4th tradition celebrating America's 250th birthday.

events.title: Redwood City 4th of July Parade 2026
events.description: Northern California's largest Independence Day parade winds 1.3 miles through historic downtown Redwood City starting at 10 a.m. This free, family-friendly July 4th tradition features marching bands, floats, and community groups celebrating America's 250th birthday. Arrive early for a great curbside spot along Broadway.

events.title: Sonoma Hometown 4th of July Parade & Fireworks 2026 - Sonoma Plaza
events.description: Sonoma's Hometown 4th of July Parade & Fireworks 2026 fills the historic Sonoma Plaza with an old-fashioned parade, live music, food vendors and a kid-friendly splash zone, capped by a 50+ year fireworks tradition at dusk. This free, family-friendly celebration has been named one of the best July 4th events in the country.

events.title: El Cerrito worldOne 4th of July Festival 2026 (Free)
events.description: Celebrate the Fourth of July in El Cerrito at the free City of El Cerrito & worldOne Festival in Cerrito Vista Park, 11 a.m. to 5 p.m. This multicultural family festival features world-class music, a classic car showcase, kids' rides, bounce houses and food booths. Free admission with pay-as-you-go attractions.

events.title: Marin County Fair 2026: July 4th Fireworks & Carnival - San Rafael
events.description: Spend the Fourth of July at the 2026 Marin County Fair in San Rafael, packed with carnival rides, live music, exhibits and family-friendly fun. Admission includes free July 4th fireworks over the lagoon, making this a marquee all-ages Independence Day destination in Marin.
events.cost: Admission ticket required (includes rides, concerts & fireworks)

events.title: San Mateo 4th of July in the Park 2026 at Central Park
